# UC-10.18.9 · Broadcom Symantec CloudSOC Shadow IT Signals via DLP Cloud Destinations

## Description

CloudSOC programs often start with telemetry about unsanctioned applications. DLP incidents targeting consumer cloud destinations provide a complementary, data-centric view of shadow IT—where employees actually move files—not just where they browse.

## Value

Prioritizes SaaS governance by highlighting high-volume or repeat offenders using non-approved collaboration tools, distinct from pure web-proxy CASB views.

## Implementation

(1) Ensure DLP incidents include destination or channel metadata. (2) Curate regex lists for consumer SaaS domains. (3) Join to an approved-SaaS registry lookup to reduce noise. (4) Hand off top policies to architecture review. (5) Do not duplicate pure WSS-only dashboards; use this for exfil-focused metrics.

## SPL

```spl
index=dlp sourcetype="symantec:dlp:incident" earliest=-30d
| eval dest=coalesce(Destination, destination, Channel, channel, Application, application, URL, url, "")
| eval policy=coalesce(Policy_Name, policy_name, "")
| where match(lower(dest), "(?i)dropbox|wetransfer|personal|consumer|unknown.*cloud|file.share|sendspace") OR match(lower(policy), "(?i)cloud|saas|unsanctioned|shadow")
| stats count values(dest) as destinations dc(user) as unique_users by policy
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (incidents by policy), table (policy, unique users, sample destinations), trend line.

## References

- [Broadcom Symantec Information Protection — DLP](https://www.broadcom.com/products/cyber-security/information-protection/data-loss-prevention)
- [Splunk Lantern](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
